perturbation.original_text: Each Party shall take reasonable security precautions to protect the Confidential Information, at least as strict as the precautions it takes to protect its own confidential and proprietary information of a similar nature.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party shall take minimal security precautions to protect the Confidential Information, at least as strict as the precautions it takes to protect its own confidential and proprietary information of a similar nature.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'reasonable security precautions' to 'minimal security precautions' in Section 3 creates an **in-text contradiction**. The modified term is now vague and undefined, making enforcement difficult. Furthermore, this affects Section 7's remedy provision, as it is unclear what constitutes a breach in the light of 'minimal precautions'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Remedies for Breach. The Parties agree that money damages would be inadequate to remedy any breach of this Agreement. As a result, a non-breaching party shall be entitled to seek, and a court of competent jurisdiction may grant, specific performance and 2 injunctive or other equitable relief as a remedy for any breach of this Agreement. Such remedy shall be in addition to all other remedies, including money damages, available to a non-breaching party at law or in equity.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'reasonable security precautions' to 'minimal security precautions' introduces ambiguity, making it difficult to determine what constitutes a breach. This directly contradicts the 'Remedies for Breach' section, as it's unclear how the remedy provisions can be applied when the standard for security is undefined and 'minimal'. The original text had a standard that while open to some interpretation, provided a baseline for a breach, and now that baseline is severely altered, which affects enforcement of the Remedies for Breach section, therefore a contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: The obligations of confidentiality and protection under this Agreement shall survive for three (3) years following the expiration of the Term.
perturbation.changed_text: The obligations of confidentiality and protection under this Agreement shall survive for one (1) year following the expiration of the Term.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'three (3) years' to 'one (1) year' directly contradicts the existing timeframe for confidentiality obligations after the term expires. Given the business purpose of strategic merger, acquisition, or business combination, this creates an **in-text contradiction** about the agreement's scope.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non-Disclosure and Confidentiality . The Recipient shall not (a) use the Confidential Information except for the Business Purpose, or (b) disclose or make the Confidential Information available to any person or entity (other than Related Parties, as defined below) without the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party. Each Party shall take minimal security precautions to protect the Confidential Information, at least as strict as the precautions it takes to protect its own confidential and proprietary information of a similar nature. Without the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party, the Recipient of Confidential Information shall restrict the disclosure and availability of Confidential Information to Recipient’s financial, tax, or legal advisors, or to its directors, officers, or employees with a demonstrable need to know such Confidential Information (each, a “ Related Party ”, and, collectively, the “ Related Parties ”), provided that any such Related Party either agrees to be bound by this Agreement or is already bound by confidentiality obligations no less restrictive than those in this Agreement. Each Party shall be responsible for the breach of this Agreement by any Related Party. Neither Party shall use any Confidential Information received by it to develop a product or service which competes with or imitates products of the Disclosing Party or engage in reverse engineering to develop similar products or services using the Confidential Information (it being acknowledged and understood that both Parties are engaged in the business of beer brewing and that competing beer products are regularly independently developed within the beer industry using the same or similar raw materials, processes and packaging). The Recipient shall prevent commingling of the Confidential Information with similar information or material of other third parties. The obligations of confidentiality and protection under this Agreement shall survive for one (1) year following the expiration of the Term.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a 3-year survival period for confidentiality obligations, whereas the changed text modifies this to 1 year. The contradicted text contains the statement "The obligations of confidentiality and protection under this Agreement shall survive for one (1) year following the expiration of the Term." as well. Because the changed text also includes "The obligations of confidentiality and protection under this Agreement shall survive for one (1) year following the expiration of the Term", this leads to a direct in-text contradiction because the original version provided for a three year term, according to the explanation provided.

perturbation.original_text: A prevailing Party shall be entitled to an award of its reasonable attorneys’ fees and costs arising out of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: A prevailing Party may be entitled to an award of its reasonable attorneys’ fees and costs arising out of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall be entitled' to 'may be entitled' in Section 9 alters the certainty of awarding attorney's fees to the prevailing party in a dispute. This introduction of ambiguity directly impacts Section 7's remedy provisions by making the cost of enforcing the agreement uncertain, creating an **in-text contradiction** about the extent of available remedies.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Remedies for Breach. The Parties agree that money damages would be inadequate to remedy any breach of this Agreement. As a result, a non-breaching party shall be entitled to seek, and a court of competent jurisdiction may grant, specific performance and 2 injunctive or other equitable relief as a remedy for any breach of this Agreement. Such remedy shall be in addition to all other remedies, including money damages, available to a non-breaching party at law or in equity.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that a prevailing party 'shall be entitled' to attorney's fees, creating a clear entitlement. The changed text modifies this to 'may be entitled,' introducing ambiguity. This directly contradicts the 'Remedies for Breach' section, which asserts that money damages are inadequate and that a non-breaching party 'shall be entitled' to certain remedies. By making attorney's fees uncertain, the changed text creates an in-text contradiction regarding the extent and availability of remedies, as it makes the cost recovery uncertain, thus affecting the full remedy available.
